countries/central-african-republic.summary: Central African Republic: touched by crisis-states-t2 batch; no entity-specific compassion-relevant evidence surfaced in last 14 days.

countries/south-sudan.summary: HRW May 6: civilians starving in conflict areas. IPC: 7.8M (56% population) acutely food insecure April-July 2026; 73,300 in IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe (160% increase). Luakpiny/Nasir County at famine risk. 142,000 displaced from Akobo County; 100,000 fled to Ethiopia. WFP requires $266M urgently. Fighting between SAF and SPLM-IO escalated.

countries/somalia.summary: UN agencies confirmed 6M (31% of population) facing IPC Phase 3+ April-June 2026; 1.9M in Emergency. Burhakaba District IPC Phase 5 with famine risk. 1.9M children acute malnutrition; 493,000 severe. 2026 HNRP only 15.2% funded. Middle East conflict pushed food prices up 60%. First famine risk since 2022.

countries/afghanistan.summary: Taliban Decree No. 18 promulgated May 14 codifies child marriage — UNAMA confirmed May 21 that provisions imply permissibility of child marriage (silence = consent). 21.9M require humanitarian assistance. Aid down 16.5% in 2025; 440+ clinics closed. 66 OCHA access incidents in April. Security Council June 2026 Monthly Forecast published.

countries/north-korea.summary: DPRK deployed soldiers to Russia for Ukraine war in exchange for oil and nuclear/missile technology. PBS/defectors: starvation and executions reported inside DPRK. South Korea March 2026: renamed "defectors" to "North Korean-born citizens." Women face trafficking/exploitation if fleeing. North Korea last assessed May 17.

countries/myanmar.summary: Junta airstrike May 21: fighter jet and suicide drone killed 4 civilians, destroyed 20+ homes in Khin-U Township, Sagaing region. 1,000+ civilian locations hit in 15 months; 1,728 civilians killed by airstrikes since late 2024. 3.6M displaced (record). 12M face acute hunger. Airstrikes target schools, churches, medical facilities.

countries/haiti.summary: 30,000 newly displaced in last 10 days from fresh gang violence waves (May 2026). 1.4M total displaced including 741,000 children. 90% Port-au-Prince under gang control.
